parameters.query:
SELECT
  COALESCE((ev.parsed_json->>'relevance_score')::int, 0)     AS relevance_score,
  CASE
    WHEN j.source_url LIKE '%xing.com%'            THEN 'XING'
    WHEN j.source_url LIKE '%linkedin.com%'         THEN 'LinkedIn'
    WHEN j.source_url LIKE '%berlinstartupjobs%'    THEN 'BerlinStartupJobs'
    WHEN j.source_url LIKE '%indeed.%'              THEN 'Indeed'
    WHEN j.source_url LIKE '%greenhouse.io%'        THEN 'Greenhouse'
    WHEN j.source_url LIKE '%lever.co%'             THEN 'Lever'
    WHEN j.source_url LIKE '%arbeitnow%'            THEN 'Arbeitnow'
    WHEN j.source_url LIKE '%remotive%'             THEN 'Remotive'
    WHEN j.source_url LIKE '%weworkremotely%'       THEN 'WeWorkRemotely'
    WHEN j.source_url LIKE '%remotely.de%'          THEN 'Remotely.de'
    WHEN j.source_url LIKE '%devjobs.de%'           THEN 'DevJobs'
    WHEN j.source_input::text = 'manual'            THEN 'Manual'
    ELSE regexp_replace(j.source_url, '^https?://(?:www\.)?([^/?#]+).*$', '\1')
  END                                                        AS source_site,
  c.name                                                     AS company,
  j.job_title,
  j.seniority::text,
  j.location,
  j.work_mode::text,
  j.current_stage::text,
  j.summary,
  j.source_url,
  j.created_at::text
FROM jobs j
JOIN companies c ON c.company_id = j.company_id
JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT parsed_json
  FROM job_events
  WHERE job_id = j.id
  ORDER BY created_at DESC
  LIMIT 1
) ev ON TRUE
WHERE (j.seniority IS NULL OR j.seniority NOT IN ('senior', 'lead', 'principal', 'staff', 'head', 'executive'))
ORDER BY relevance_score DESC, j.created_at DESC;

parameters.jsCode:
const jobs = $input.all().map(item => item.json);
return [{ json: { jobs } }];
